source.section: Part 10 - Offence: Weapons & Armament > 2 - Air-to-Ground Weapons > 2.8 - AGM-65E Maverick (Laser-Guided MAV)

steps.action: Master Mode – A/G (switch can only be activated when wheels are off the ground)
steps.voiced: Press A/G Master Mode — must be airborne.

steps.action: Master Arm switch – ARM (UP)
steps.voiced: Set Master Arm switch to ARM.

steps.action: Go in SMS (Stores Management System) page
steps.voiced: Select the SMS page.

steps.action: Select MAV (text should be boxed) by pressing the OSB next to MAV. Press it a second time to enter the MAV DSPLY (Maverick Display) page.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to MAV to select it, then press again to enter the MAV DSPLY page.

steps.action: Laser Maverick will start a self-test that takes about 30 seconds. You can monitor cooldown progress in the MAV DSPLY page by pressing the OSB next to the crossed-out « MAV » from the SMS (Stores Management System) page. (Note: Once Maverick cooldown is complete, the timer will disappear.)
steps.voiced: Confirm Laser Maverick self-test begins — takes ~30 seconds; timer clears when complete.

steps.action: Select INST (Instantaneous) Fuzing.
steps.voiced: Select INST fuzing.

steps.action: In the MAV DSPLY page, press the OSB next to UFC (Up-Front Controller). This will select all Laser-Maverick stations (boxed when selected) and allow us to edit their laser codes all at once. If you want to edit a specific Maverick's laser code, press UFC again to switch between stations.
steps.voiced: In the MAV DSPLY page, press the OSB next to UFC to edit laser codes for all stations.

steps.action: Press the button next to “CODE” (Laser-Guided Maverick Laser Code). A “:” will indicate it is selected. Then, enter the desired laser code on the keypad and press “ENT”. In our case, we will choose a laser code 1456, which we will set up for our targeting pod as well in later steps. (Note: Keep in mind that if we were tracking) someone else's laser, we would need to set the laser code to the one used by their own targeting pod.
steps.voiced: Press UFC button next to CODE, enter the desired laser code, then press ENT.

steps.action: To power up the targeting pod, set FLIR Sensor Switch to STBY (Right Click).
steps.voiced: Set FLIR Sensor Switch to STBY to begin targeting pod warm-up.

steps.action: From the TAC page on either DDI or the AMPCD, press the OSB (Option Select Button) next to “FLIR” (Forward-Looking Infrared) to select the Targeting Pod feed page. Monitor the warm-up process. Targeting pod warm-up process will take about 2 minutes. When targeting pod is ready to be used, the pod status will switch from RDY – NOT TIMED OUT to STBY (Standby).
steps.voiced: Select the FLIR page — monitor warm-up; pod status switches to STBY when ready in ~2 minutes.

steps.action: Set FLIR Sensor Switch to ON (Right Click). Confirm that Targeting Pod mode switches from STBY to OPR (Operate).
steps.voiced: Set FLIR Sensor Switch to ON — confirm pod switches from STBY to OPR.

steps.action: Press the Sensor Control Switch in the direction of the selected DDI (Right for the Right DDI as an example). The Select Focus Diamond indicates what display is selected.
steps.voiced: Press Sensor Control Switch toward the selected DDI — confirm Select Focus Diamond appears.

steps.action: Press the Undesignate button twice rapidly to select VVSLV (Velocity Vector Slaved) mode. This will un-stow the camera and slave the targeting pod to the velocity vector.
steps.voiced: Press the Undesignate button twice to select VVSLV mode.

steps.action: Press the Undesignate button twice rapidly again to select Snowplow mode. When Snowplow mode is first entered, the FLIR is set to 0° left/right and -8° down.
steps.voiced: Press Undesignate twice again to enter Snowplow mode — FLIR initializes to 0° azimuth and -8°.

steps.action: Set LTD/R (Laser Target Designator/Ranger) switch to ARM. This will arm the laser. Confirm that L ARM indication is displayed on the FLIR page. Verify A/G Master Mode is set since it will allow you to arm the laser designator.
steps.voiced: Set LTD/R switch to ARM — confirm L ARM appears on the FLIR page.

steps.action: Using the TDC (Throttle Designator Controller) Aft/Fwd/Left/Right controls, slew the targeting pod reticle over the desired target.
steps.voiced: Use TDC Aft/Fwd/Left/Right to slew the pod reticle over the target.

steps.action: Use appropriate zoom level, field of view (NARROW/WIDE) and camera mode (CCD/TV or FLIR) to identify the target.
steps.voiced: Set zoom level, FOV, and camera mode as required to identify the target.

steps.action: Press the Sensor Control Switch Towards Selected Display (Right if our right DDI is selected) to toggle between Point Track (PTRK, tracks a moving object like a high-contrast vehicle) and Area Track (ATRK, used for a static target).
steps.voiced: Press Sensor Control Switch toward selected display to toggle Point Track or Area Track.

steps.action: Use the TDC (Throttle Designator Controller) Depress button to designate the laser as the target point. This will slave selected air- to-ground weapons to where the laser is firing and display range to target and its coordinates.
steps.voiced: Press TDC DEPRESS to designate the laser as the target point.

steps.action: Press the OSB next to “UFC” to set the Targeting Pod laser code on the Up-Front Controller.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to UFC to set the targeting pod laser code.

steps.action: Press the button next to “LTDC” (Laser Target Designator Code). A “:” will indicate it is selected. Then, enter the desired laser code on the keypad and press “ENT”. We will choose laser code 1456, which we set previously in step 8 for the laser Mavericks themselves.
steps.voiced: Press UFC button next to LTDC, enter the laser code to match the Maverick, then press ENT.

steps.action: Now that the targeting pod has its laser code and it matches the laser code tracked by the Laser-Guided Maverick, we can now uncage the missile to make it track the laser.
steps.voiced: Confirm targeting pod laser code matches the Maverick laser code — ready to uncage.

steps.action: On the FLIR page, make sure the OSB next to “TRIG” in unboxed (not selected). This means that the targeting pod will automatically lase (auto-lasing) just before the AGM-65E Maverick is fired. Laser Armed
steps.voiced: Confirm TRIG is unboxed — targeting pod will auto-lase just before missile fires.

steps.action: While the sensor of interest is the targeting pod (Sensor Focus diamond is on the FLIR page), the Maverick remains caged and boresighted.
steps.voiced: Confirm Maverick remains caged while targeting pod is the sensor of interest.

steps.action: The crossed-out MAV indicates that the Maverick is not tracking any laser yet, which is normal since the targeting pod has not started firing its laser yet.
steps.voiced: Confirm crossed-out MAV is normal — laser not yet firing.

steps.action: The TTMR indicates the time in seconds until the aircraft becomes within range to be able to fire the maverick.
steps.voiced: Confirm TTMR shows time until within firing range.

steps.action: Select desired Maverick station using the OSB next to STEP. We will select Station 8.
steps.voiced: Select desired Maverick station using the OSB next to STEP.

steps.action: Set Sensor Control Switch to the Maverick Display page's DDI (Sensor Control Switch LEFT since we showed up the MAV DSPLY feed on the left DDI). A focus diamond will appear to show the left DDI is selected.
steps.voiced: Set Sensor Control Switch toward the MAV DSPLY DDI — confirm focus diamond appears.

steps.action: Press the Cage/Uncage Button to uncage the Maverick.
steps.voiced: Press the Cage/Uncage Button to uncage the Maverick.

steps.action: When uncaged, the missile will scan ahead in a pattern to search for the nearest laser with the code we entered earlier (1456), then slave itself to the target designated by the targeting pod if available. (Note: If for some reason the missile is caged back, press the Cage/Uncage Button to uncage the Maverick once more.)
steps.voiced: Confirm uncaged Maverick scans for matching laser code then slaves to targeting pod designation.

steps.action: Once you are within firing range (IN RNG cue will become visible on the HUD), hold the Weapon Release Button (« RALT+SPACE ») to launch missile.
steps.voiced: Hold Weapon Release Button when IN RNG cue appears to launch missile.

steps.action: Once missile is fired, the targeting pod will automatically fire its laser on the designation point in order to guide the Maverick.
steps.voiced: Confirm targeting pod auto-lases at designation point after missile fires.

steps.action: Once laser is firing, the laser mode will switch from L ARM to LTD/R.
steps.voiced: Confirm laser mode switches from L ARM to LTD/R.

steps.action: The missile will follow the laser until impact.
steps.voiced: Confirm missile follows laser until impact.

steps.action: Once the target is destroyed, the LTD/R (Laser Target Designator/Ranger) will automatically go back to SAFE. You will need to set it back to ARM if you want to lase more targets.
steps.voiced: Confirm LTD/R returns to SAFE after target destroyed — re-arm to lase additional targets.

steps.action: If you want to undesignated the target, use the Undesignate/Nosewheel Steering button on the stick.
steps.voiced: Press the Undesignate/Nosewheel Steering button to clear target designation.
